title: Shanghai International Circuit
sub: A Tilke landmark shaped like the character 上 (shàng, "upward"): a hypnotic decreasing-radius opening spiral, a kilometre-plus back straight, and one of the calendar’s best overtaking hairpins.

sectors.name: Sector 1
sectors.char: The hypnotic decreasing-radius Turn 1-3 spiral, then Turn 4-6. Front-limited and patient.

sectors.name: Sector 2
sectors.char: Fast Turn 7-8 esses and the long Turn 11-13 right that loads the tyres before the back straight.

sectors.name: Sector 3
sectors.char: The 1.2km back straight, the heavy Turn 14 hairpin, then technical final turns.

tyreAlloc: Middle of the range · C2 / C3 / C4
stratNote: The long Turn 1-3 complex and Turn 7-8 esses stress the front-left. On a fresh surface with limited running, front graining is the key variable.
spots: End of the 1.2km back straight + DRS. One of the best passing spots on the calendar.
Heavy braking after the back-section run; a strong secondary move.
Off the main straight + DRS into the spiral; opening-lap action.
otNote: The huge back straight into the slow Turn 14 hairpin is a classic slam-the-brakes zone. Two DRS zones and big braking events make it reliably good.
corners: Fast entry off the straight + DRS, beginning the decreasing-radius spiral.
Keeps tightening as speed bleeds away; patience and a clean line.
Snaps left out of the spiral; drive matters.
Medium right continuing the opening complex.
Quick left linking toward the back section.
Heavy braking zone; a genuine secondary spot.
Quick, satisfying left-right esses at high speed.
Tight left at the far point of the lap.
Short right setting up the long right.
A long, sustained right onto the back straight; exit speed critical.
End of the 1.2km straight + DRS. Best overtaking spot on the lap.
Final technical rights launching onto the main straight.
geo: Tilke traced the character 上 (shàng, "above/upward"). Built for $450M in 18 months on swampy, piled ground.
A decreasing-radius right that keeps tightening as you lose speed, then flicks left. Demanding on the front-left.
Over 1.2km flat-out to the Turn 14 hairpin, one of the longest in F1. ~325 km/h, prime DRS overtaking.
Early-season cool temps, possible rain and a green surface, tricky on a Sprint weekend with one practice.
